verdict_reason: The verse closes the conditional opened in 2.26. Shankara reads it as completing the materialist concession; Ramanuja sharpens the argument by treating origination and destruction as states of an existent entity, not events that befall it. The engineering layer follows Ramanuja's structural-paired reading ('both heir and ancestor') without claiming Shankara also held this reading. The translators' interpretive disagreement (Prabhupada injecting 'duty,' Mukundananda staying literal) is recorded honestly. The verse stays on the metaphysical-inevitability leg of the argument; the engineering layer respects this by not yet pivoting to duty (that pivot is the 2.30/2.31 boundary).
